Publisher · Storyteller · Educator

Where stories become legacy.

The Arena of Knowledge is the home of everything Ayush Kumar Mishra builds — novels, worldbuilding, and the ideas behind them. It currently houses Project Trident, a five-volume science-fantasy saga, alongside a growing library of history, science, and literature content. More titles will live here as they're written.

"Children often ask how our world began. They expect tales of emperors, gods, and ancient kingdoms. The truth is far stranger. Our history began with a dying world, a broken ship, three brothers, and Hope." — From the Imperial Archives of Vaikunth-1, Year 7817 A.R.
Featured Work

Project Trident — currently being written

A five-volume science-fantasy saga. Earth was dying. Three brothers were stranded on the alien world of Vaikunth-1. What began as a survival story became the founding myth of an empire.

On the Channel

The Arena of Knowledge — YouTube

History, science, literature, and astronomy — told to make sense of the world, one video at a time.

Watch the latest →

Everything published or in progress under The Arena of Knowledge lives here. Right now that's one saga — more titles, series, and standalone works will be added to this shelf as they're written.

Fictional Universes

Every self-contained universe, saga, and world Ayush Kumar Mishra builds lives here — each with its own physics, mythology, and civilizations. Explore the current flagship below; more universes will take their place on this page as they're written.

Flagship Universe · Est. Ongoing

Project TRIDENT

The World of Vaikunth-1

A five-volume science-fantasy saga set on an alien world engineered to feel like paradise — home to the Vanara and Simha peoples, the Titanwood forest, and the human astronaut Arnav Singh, whose arrival sets a founding myth in motion. Hard planetary science meets Sanskrit-rooted mythology across a novel and its companion encyclopedias.

Science Fantasy Hard Worldbuilding Novel + Encyclopedia Sanskrit-Inspired Mythos
Enter Project TRIDENT →
What Comes Next

More Universes, In Time

New self-contained worlds will be added here as they're written and refined. Each will carry its own physics, calendar, mythology, and cast — built with the same care as Vaikunth-1.

Actively being written
Project Trident · Volume I

Age of Founders

Earth was running out of time. Project TRIDENT was sent beyond the stars to find a future — and it was never meant to end in disaster. When the mission is destroyed, three brothers are stranded on Vaikunth-1 with no way home and no one coming to save them. The world they discover is unlike anything humanity has known: vast continents, civilizations rising and falling beneath unfamiliar moons, and something ancient, watching, waiting beneath it all. To survive, they must unite rivals, forge fragile alliances, and build something greater than themselves — and every choice they make will shape the destiny of nations yet unborn.

The Founders

Three brothers, three paths

Separated during the planetary collapse, each brother rebuilds in his own way — and each becomes central to the world Vaikunth-1 is still becoming.

Leader

Arnav

Evolutionary biologist, chemist, and martial artist. Enters Simha territory as an outsider and rises to become Emperor of Vaikunth-1.

Restorer

Advik

Engineer and metallurgist. Rebuilds sacred industry from the ruins he finds, and becomes an architect of civilization in his own right.

Keeper

Atharv

Physicist and strategist. Preserves memory and knowledge where others would let it be lost, and becomes a general and statesman.

Field Roster

Key Characters

The full cast — heroes, companions, kingdoms, and villains — now lives on one page. Explore the complete Characters roster.

Sacred Law

The Marriage Bonds

Across Vaikunth-1, marriage often seals a political or sacred bond between a founder and a civilization — not merely a romance. Each brother's bonds tie him to a different people.

Arnav's Bonds

Five Lineages

Dhriti — Simha (Lion) Kingdoms
Ira — Garuda lineage
Varunika — Whale/Ocean lineage
Anantha — Naga lineage
Varija — Elephant lineage

Advik's Bonds

Three Lineages

Agneya — Wolf lineage
Ashvini — Horse lineage
Vrinda — Hansa lineage

Atharv's Bonds

Three Lineages

Shyama — Raven lineage
Nandita — Bull lineage
Varuni — Crocodile lineage

— The World of Vaikunth-1 —
The Vaikunth-1 Encyclopedia

The World of Vaikunth-1

A super-habitable world built on real astrophysics, geology, and ecology — the same planet at the center of the Trident saga, catalogued across a six-volume in-world Encyclopedia. Here's a first look.

Global Overview

Map of Vaikunth-1

Six continents, six dominant biomes, twenty-nine civilizations. Click a continent to explore its geography and peoples.

Vaikunth-1 is 2.53× Earth's surface area with 58% ocean coverage.

Fig. 1 — The Star

Ananta Shesha

Vaikunth-1's sun is a K0V orange dwarf — slightly cooler and less massive than Earth's sun, but with a main-sequence lifetime of 15–20 billion years and a spectrum favorable to photosynthesis. It formed roughly seven billion years ago from the collapse of the Ananta Nebula, a molecular cloud already rich in the pre-biotic chemistry — water, amino acids, sugar and nucleobase precursors — that life would later be built from.

Fig. 2 — The Solar System

Nine Worlds of Ananta Shesha

Vaikunth-1 is the third of nine major planets, sitting at the precise center of the star's habitable zone.

Planets & Moons

The Nine Worlds, In Full

Fig. 2 — The Solar System

Nine Worlds of Ananta Shesha

Vaikunth-1 is the third of nine major planets, sitting at the precise center of the star's habitable zone. Tap a category to explore.

0.35 AU

Meru — The First World

The innermost planet, baked at roughly seven times Vaikunth-1's stellar radiation. A bare, airless world stripped of everything volatile.

0.58 AU

Mandara — The Churning World

Sits just inside the habitable zone's inner edge — too hot for liquid water, but geologically complex and active.

1.03 AU

Vaikunth-1 — The Living Jewel

1.59× Earth's radius, 2.55× Earth's mass, yet almost exactly Earth's surface gravity — thanks to a water-rich, lower-density mantle. 2.53× Earth's surface area, positioned at the exact center of the habitable zone.

1.75 AU

Mainak — The Frontier World

A substantial rocky world at the outer edge of the terrestrial zone, roughly 3 Earth masses, marking the transition toward the giant planets.

4.5 AU

Padmanabha — Lord of Giants

The system's gravitational colossus — 0.8 Jupiter masses — whose gravity has shaped the orbits and long-term habitability of every world closer to the star.

8 AU

Amaravati — The Ringed Giant

A true gas giant of 0.45 Jupiter masses, completing its orbit in roughly 23 Earth years.

12 AU

Pushkara — The Sapphire Giant

The first of three ice giants — a deep mantle of compressed water, ammonia, and methane around a small rocky core.

17 AU

Kailasa — The Frozen Throne

Upper-atmosphere temperatures below −200°C, with a pale silver-white, blue-banded atmosphere of frozen clouds.

24 AU

Kshira — The Ocean of Milk

The outermost major planet — 125 Earth years per orbit — and by most accounts the most visually magnificent world in the system after Amaravati's rings.

Locked in a stable 1:2:4:8 orbital resonance — a real astronomical mechanism used to justify a sky where two, three, or four moons can appear at once.

Moon

Shankha

The brightest of the four moons, formed from reflective feldspar-rich material.

Moon

Chakra

The copper moon and keeper of time — its 28-day cycle anchors the Vaikunth-1 calendar.

Moon

Gada

The most massive and most distant moon — not born of Vaikunth-1, but captured from elsewhere in the system.

Moon

Padma

Master of the tides — formed from a giant impact early in the planet's history, and the dominant force behind Vaikunth-1's tidal system.

Fig. 2 — Geography

The Continents of Vaikunth-1

Six landmasses and one maritime realm — twenty-nine civilizations catalogued across them. Click a continent to explore its rivers, mountains, wonders, and peoples.

Golden Heartland

Sauryabhumi

Largest continent and historic center of civilization — vast plains, great rivers, ancient kingdoms. Home to the Simha, Vyaghra, and Vanara.

Explore Sauryabhumi →

Water Continent

Aardralok

Rivers, rainforests, and wetlands — the most biologically productive region on the planet.

Explore Aardralok →

Desert Realm

Vishadmaru

Vast deserts and highland plateaus, linked by ancient oasis networks and caravan routes.

Explore Vishadmaru →

Great Wilderness

Mahaaranya

The densest forests in the world, and some of its oldest ecological reserves.

Explore Mahaaranya →

Fjord Continent

Shvetkhand

Deep fjords and rugged coastlines — birthplace of the world's great seafaring traditions.

Explore Shvetkhand →

Volcanic Frontier

Kaaldweep

The smallest, most geologically active, and least explored continent — the planet's greatest geographic mystery.

Explore Kaaldweep →

Maritime Realm

Saagar Sangh

Vaikunth-1's oceans — home to the great naval and maritime civilizations, and the largest reef systems on the planet.

Explore Saagar Sangh →

Golden Heartland

Sauryabhumi

Largest continent and historic center of civilization — vast plains, great rivers, ancient kingdoms. Home to the Simha, Vyaghra, and Vanara.

River

Hiranyadhara

The largest river of the continent — backbone of its agriculture and trade, "the River of the Heartland."

River

Chandrika

Drains the northwest; seasonal flooding maintains long-term soil fertility.

River

Sharada

Links inland farming communities to Trident Sea maritime trade.

River

Vasudhara

Feeds the Hiranyadhara watershed from the Vajrashila Highlands.

River

Kalindi

Waters the productive agricultural lowlands of eastern Sauryabhumi.

Range

Hiranya Parvat

The highest peaks in the Northern Crown — a water tower sustaining millions downstream.

Range

Garud Shila Range

A major watershed feeding the great rivers of the Heartland, and home to important trade passes.

Highlands

Vajrashila Highlands

Rainfall-shaping uplands rich in mineral wealth and river sources.

Lake

Hiranyagarbha Lake

The largest lake in the Golden Heartland — a central freshwater reservoir.

Kingdom

Simha Kingdoms

The Imperial Builders — political center of Vaikunth-1 for millennia. Queen Dhriti's people.

Dynasty

Vyaghra Dynasty

Where the Simha represent order, the Vyaghra represent freedom — feared warrior-frontier civilization.

Clans

Chitraka Clans

Masters of stealth and wilderness survival — a decentralized clan society built on independence and harmony with nature.

Confederacy

Vega Confederacy

A commerce-driven alliance whose transport networks and innovation connect distant civilizations across continents.

Tribes

Garuda Tribes

Fearless explorers and navigators whose expeditions have expanded the known world.

Kul

Shyen Kul

Guardians of the sky — occupy the high mountain passes and frontier peaks.

Circles

Vanar Circles

The most creative and improvisational of all — the Vanara who first guide the brothers on Vaikunth-1.

Water Continent

Aardralok

Rivers, rainforests, and wetlands — the most biologically productive region on the planet.

River

Meghja

Largest river of the Water Continent — a major transportation corridor rivaling the Hiranyadhara in importance.

River

Iravati

Sustains extensive floodplain farming communities in western Aardralok.

River

Adrija

Winds through wetlands of central and southern Aardralok — a biodiversity hotspot.

River

Makarvahini

Principal feeder of the Makar Delta, carrying massive sediment loads.

Range

Shashvat Range

The principal water tower of the Water Continent — source of its major rivers.

Sea

Jalasar

The largest inland freshwater sea on Vaikunth-1 — a transportation hub and fisheries center so vast it feels like an inland ocean.

Delta

Makar Delta

The largest delta on the planet — dense population, deep fertility, and centuries of civilizational wealth.

Wetlands

Rohini Wetlands

The largest wetland complex in Aardralok — a major biodiversity hotspot and flood regulator.

Sabha

Kaak Sabha

Where the Naga preserve knowledge, the Kaak preserve memory — keepers of the oldest archives.

Hives

Madhukar Hives

Deepest connection to agriculture and ecology of any civilization on the planet.

Empire

Anthill Empire

Master builders — roads, canals, bridges, dams, and flood-control infrastructure.

Union

Rodent Union

The most adaptable people on Vaikunth-1, having outlasted wars, floods, and upheavals.

Dynasty

Makar Dynasty

Built the waterways — river cities and inland trade fleets of great economic power.

Desert Realm

Vishadmaru

Vast deserts and highland plateaus, linked by ancient oasis networks and caravan routes.

River

Vaitarni

The most important river in Vishadmaru — sustains life across the entire desert continent.

River

Shonitdhara

Supports settlements and agriculture through the arid zones.

River

Lavana

Feeds the Lavan Kund salt basin, critical during dry seasons.

Range

Raktashila Range

The continent's dominant mountains — their rain shadow creates the desert itself.

Plateau

Griddhamukh Plateau

Dramatic cliffs and specialized ecosystems in the continent's northwest.

Salt Flat

Lavan Kund

The continent's largest salt flat — a mirror-like reflective surface after seasonal rains.

Oasis

Nagasrot Oasis

An underground-fed oasis and historic caravan stopping point on desert trade routes.

Clan

Naga Clan

The thinkers of Vaikunth-1 — scholars, astronomers, and physicians of great renown.

Clan

Vrischika Clan

Forged by the harshest desert conditions on the planet — masters of survival.

Tribes

Gridha Tribes

Keepers of memory through stone — bound to ruins, tombs, and lost cities.

Federation

Lomash Federation

Masters of commerce, diplomacy, and finance across every continent.

Wonder

Raktashikhar

The highest mountain of the Desert Continent — red-colored, and a critical water source for surrounding regions.

Great Wilderness

Mahaaranya

The densest forests in the world, and some of its oldest ecological reserves.

River

Aranyavahini

Largest river of the Great Wilderness — connects ecological zones and serves as a major transport corridor.

River

Somprabha

Drains the Somgiri Plateau, stabilizing ecology across large areas.

River

Neelparna

Flows through the central forest — a highly biodiverse river basin.

Range

Aranyadeep Range

The hydrological foundation of the entire continent.

Shield

Adishila Shield

An ancient craton — the oldest exposed rocks on the planet, holding billions of years of geological record.

Wetlands

Maral Wetlands

One of the largest wetland systems on the planet.

Clans

Wolf Clans

The industrial engine of Vaikunth-1 — Agneya's people, guardians of sacred forges.

Confederacy

Hansa Confederacy

Civilization's philosophers — wisdom, ethics, and literature above all.

Court

Mayur Court

Shaped art, architecture, music, and aesthetics more than any other people.

Republic

Ashwa Republic

Masters of movement — roads, riders, and courier networks connecting the world.

Protectorate

Rhino Protectorate

Where others build kingdoms, the Rhino build fortresses — defense and military engineering.

Wonder

Vajradhara Falls

The largest waterfall on Vaikunth-1 — mist clouds visible from great distances, a symbol of the wilderness's hydrological power.

Wonder

Aranya Gorge

Carved by river erosion over geological time, exposing ancient rock layers and continental history.

Fjord Continent

Shvetkhand

Deep fjords and rugged coastlines — birthplace of the world's great seafaring traditions.

River

Hima River

Largest river of the Fjord Continent — mountain-sourced, shaping forest and valley ecosystems.

River

Devdhara

Drains the central highlands, heavily influenced by seasonal snowmelt.

Range

Himraj Mountains

The dominant system of the Fjord Continent — source of the glaciers that carve its fjords.

Lake

Tushar Lake

A glacial lake and major freshwater reservoir amid dramatic mountain scenery.

Delta

Hima Delta

Productive coastal wetlands supporting fisheries and agriculture.

Republic

Bear Republic

Masters of the cold — one of the most resilient civilizations on Vaikunth-1.

Mandala

Gaja Mandala

Built institutions of law, governance, and political stability.

League

Ruminant League

The shepherds of civilization — one of its most cooperative societies.

Territories

Owl Territories

Seek wisdom beneath the stars — one of the great intellectual civilizations.

Wonder

Shvetgarbh Fjord

The deepest fjord on Vaikunth-1, carved by ancient glaciers beneath towering mountain walls.

Volcanic Frontier

Kaaldweep

The smallest, most geologically active, and least explored continent — the planet's greatest geographic mystery.

Encircled by the violent Rudra Sea and abyssal trenches, Kaaldweep has resisted every expedition sent to chart it. Survivors return with fragmented accounts of impossible creatures and forgotten ruins — no confirmed civilization has ever been catalogued here.

Rudra Sea

Kaalgarbh Trench

The deepest known point on Vaikunth-1, encircling Kaaldweep — one of the least explored environments in existence.

Maritime Realm

Saagar Sangh

Vaikunth-1's oceans — home to the great naval and maritime civilizations, and the largest reef systems on the planet.

Confederacy

Orca Confederacy

Greatest maritime influence of any civilization — fleets that cross every sea.

Clan

Dolphin Clan

Seeks what lies beyond the known oceans — explorers and discoverers.

Dynasty

Shark Dynasty

Naval power and strategic dominance — the most feared fleets on the water.

Clan

Turtle Clan

Endurance above all — has outlasted empires, trade routes, and dynasties alike.

Wonder

Saagar Sangh Reefs

The largest marine reef ecosystems on the planet, sustaining the world's greatest fisheries.

Fig. 4 - Belief

The Six Great Faiths

The Six Great Faiths of Vaikunth-1 emerged primarily in the centuries following the Great Mutation. They continue to shape civilizations, laws, ethics, and daily life across the six continents.

Largest Faith

Ritam

Faith of Cosmic Order; the largest and most influential tradition.

Liberation

Nivriti

The Path of Release, centered on wisdom, detachment, and freedom from suffering.

Living World

Pranika

A nature-centered faith rooted in sacred life and the Great Living Cycle.

Sacred Truth

Tazrim

The faith of sacred truth and law, built around justice, duty, and endurance.

Lineage

Kuladra

The faith of ancestors, memory, and the unbroken Chain of Generations.

Celestial

Jyotir

Astronomy, the Four Moons, and the search for enlightenment through the heavens.

Cosmic Order

Ritam - The Faith of Cosmic Order

Core Belief: The universe operates according to an underlying Cosmic Order, Ritam. Civilization flourishes only when individuals, communities, and rulers live in harmony with it.

Central Symbol: Ananta Shesha, a composite sacred being: lion head for leadership, tiger claws for strength, panther legs for adaptability, Garuda wings for wisdom, serpent tail for eternity, and solar halo for order.

Afterlife: Reincarnation based on actions and fulfillment of duty.

Canon Notes

Pillars & Practice

Four Pillars: Dharma, Vidya, Shaurya, and Samarasya.

Key Practices: Temple worship, philosophical study, royal edicts, and festivals of order.

Sacred Sites: Temple of Ananta Shesha, Hiranyagarbha Lake, Hiranyadhara River, and Hiranya Parvat.

Colors: Gold, saffron, and crimson.

Influence: Strongly tied to governance and kingship.

Path of Release

Nivriti - The Path of Release

Founder: Nivar, the First Wanderer, a prince who renounced wealth after witnessing suffering.

Core Teaching: Suffering arises from attachment. Liberation, or Nivrita, comes through wisdom, detachment, and understanding impermanence.

Symbol: Open Circle, freedom from endless cycles.

Canon Notes

Truths & Practice

Four Truths: Life contains suffering; attachment creates suffering; freedom is possible; wisdom and discipline lead to liberation.

Practices: Meditation, monasticism, compassion, and simple living.

Sacred Animal: Crane.

Colors: White, grey, and pale blue.

Key Sites: Monastery of the First Wanderer, Himraaj Parvat, and Aardrija River.

Living World

Pranika - The Living World Faith

Core Belief: All life is sacred and interconnected in the Great Living Cycle.

Founder: No single founder; Pranika is an ancient nature-based tradition.

Key Concept: Everything returns to the living world after death.

Canon Notes

Cycle & Sacred Places

Nature Spirits: Rivers, forests, mountains, and storms are revered as living presences.

World Tree: Roots represent the past, the trunk the present, and the branches the future.

Practices: Reverence for sacred groves, rivers, and ecosystems, along with shamanic traditions.

Sacred Sites: Makar Delta, Sacred Forests of Mahaaranya, and Vajradhara Falls.

Sacred Truth

Tazrim - The Faith of Sacred Truth

Founder: Tazir the Survivor, who witnessed chaos after the Great Mutation.

Core Message: Civilizations collapse when truth is abandoned.

Symbol: Eye of Truth.

Afterlife: One life, judgment before Truth, then the Eternal Garden or the Wastes Beyond Light.

Canon Notes

Law & Judgment

Seven Virtues: Truth, discipline, duty, justice, courage, loyalty, and endurance.

Sacred Animals: Griddha, scorpion, and Naga.

Influence: Strong influence over law, justice systems, and governance.

Key Sites: Tomb of Tazir, Seven Pillars Sanctuary, and Raktashila.

Ancestors

Kuladra - The Faith of Ancestors

Core Belief: The living are part of an unbroken Chain of Generations.

Symbol: The Eternal Flame, representing the fires of past, present, and future.

Greatest Fear: Oblivion, the fate of being forgotten.

Canon Notes

Memory & Lineage

Key Concept: Ancestors continue to guide descendants.

Practices: Maintaining Lineage Chronicles, ancestor shrines, and memorial rituals.

Festivals: Night of Ancestors, Festival of Names, and Flame of Remembrance.

Sacred Sites: Ancestral Halls, Clan Tombs, and Royal Necropolises.

Celestial

Jyotir - The Celestial Faith

Core Belief: The cosmos reveals spiritual truth. Understanding the heavens brings enlightenment.

Central Focus: The Four Moons: Shankha for knowledge, Chakra for power, Gada for endurance, and Padma for wisdom.

Symbol: Four moons surrounding a central star.

Canon Notes

Moons & Observatories

Afterlife: The Celestial Journey through the Four Moons, culminating in the Great Light.

Practices: Astronomy, observation, scholarship, and observatories.

Sacred Animals: Owl, eagle, and naag.

Colors: Midnight blue, silver, and violet.

Key Sites: Great Observatory of Vishadmaru and Sauryabhumi, and Himraaj Sky Sanctuaries.

Fig. 6 — Flora & Fauna

Life Across Vaikunth-1

A biosphere reshaped by the Great Mutation — the same catastrophic event that gave rise to the planet's animal-lineage civilizations. Tap a category to explore.

Origin Event

The Great Mutation

The planet-wide transformation that reshaped Vaikunth-1's entire biosphere — the deep-history root of every animal-lineage civilization in this Encyclopedia.

Flora

Titanwoods

The largest trees on Vaikunth-1 — the tallest reach over 200 meters, each one a living ecosystem, dominating the forests of Mahaaranya.

Flora

Sungrass Family

Foundation of the open plains. Kora Grass, its ancestral species, was domesticated into the world's most important cereal crop.

Flora

Frostspire

The most widespread tree of Shvetkhand's coldwood forests, growing 40–80 meters tall and built for resilience over size.

Megafauna

Gigantus

A colossal megaherbivore of the open plains, 10–15 meters long, with three horns whose channels pulse blue-green in darkness.

Megafauna

Crownback Wanderer

A migratory giant whose seasonal movements shape the ecology of entire regions.

Megafauna

River Colossus

A massive wetland giant, as central to Aardralok's ecosystems as the Gigantus is to the plains.

2.5–3.5m

Sunmane Lion

A social apex predator of the Sauryabhumi grasslands — the sacred animal symbol of the Simha civilization.

3–4.5m

Shadowstripe Tiger

A solitary predator of the dense forests — sacred symbol of the Vyaghra Dynasty.

5–9m

Ironjaw Hunter

One of the largest terrestrial carnivores on the planet — a true super predator of forests and woodland margins.

15–30m wingspan

Sunfeather Roc

A giant aerial hunter of mountain ranges and highlands, preying on large mammals and carrion.

3–5m wingspan

Ghostwing

A large nocturnal aerial predator of the forests, pale-coated and silent in flight.

Worldwide

Sky Talon

No aerial predator is more widespread — found on every continent, a generalist raptor above all.

10–20m

Trident Hunter

The undisputed apex predator of Vaikunth-1's coastal oceans.

6–15m

Giant Tide Ray

A graceful filter-feeding giant of the open oceans, spanning up to 15 meters wingtip to wingtip.

1–2m

Reef Turtle

One of the most recognizable inhabitants of the Saagar Sangh's shallow reef seas.

— The Full Cast —
The Full Cast

Characters of Vaikunth-1

Every major figure across TRIDENT, gathered in one place.

Fig. 1 — Main Heroes
Protagonist · First Emperor

Arnav Mishra

The central protagonist of TRIDENT and the first human Emperor of Vaikunth-1. After surviving the Project Trident disaster, he unites the world's civilizations through courage, compassion, and leadership. His mysterious Void Power makes him the greatest hope against the coming darkness.

Neurobiologist

Vaidehi

A brilliant neurobiologist and the emotional heart of the story. Stranded on Vaikunth-1 months before Arnav, she survives alone while preserving hope for humanity. Her compassion, intelligence, and healing abilities inspire everyone around her.

Engineer · Metallurgist

Advik Mishra

A genius engineer and metallurgist who rebuilds civilization from forgotten ruins. He restores ancient industries, sacred forges, and lost technologies, becoming the architect of Vaikunth-1's technological renaissance.

Physicist · Strategist

Atharv Mishra

A physicist, strategist, and guardian of humanity's collective memory. He unites the Raven Archives, preserving Earth's history and guiding civilizations through knowledge instead of force.

Fig. 2 — Legendary Figure
First Survivor of Project Trident

Commander Vikram Rao

Commander of the original Project Trident mission and the first known human to survive on Vaikunth-1. Living among the Vanara for decades, he left behind journals, maps, and knowledge that would one day guide Arnav.

Fig. 3 — Vanara Civilization
Elder of Taruvan

Elder Tharun

The wise elder of Taruvan and respected leader of the Vanara people. Known for his patience and compassion, he mentors Arnav during his earliest days on Vaikunth-1.

Vanara Companion

Rudraksh

Arnav's closest Vanara friend and loyal companion. A talented craftsman with a sharp sense of humor, he introduces Arnav to the customs, traditions, and spirit of his people.

Warrior · Healer

Saanvi

An experienced warrior and skilled healer who trains Arnav to survive the dangers of Vaikunth-1. Practical, fearless, and highly respected.

Tracker · Beast-bond Warrior

Kiraat

A quiet but formidable Vanara warrior renowned for his bond with the legendary beast Tarang. A master tracker and animal handler who earns respect through action.

Fig. 4 — Simha Kingdom
Heir of the Lion Kingdom · Arnav's Queen

Queen Dhriti

The rightful heir of the Lion Kingdom and one of the greatest warriors of her generation. After being defeated by Arnav in the Coronation Trial, she becomes his queen and strongest political ally.

Commander of the Heir's Guard

Commander Kesari

The legendary commander of the Heir's Guard and one of the Lion Kingdom's finest military leaders. Fiercely devoted to his people, his loyalty is constantly tested by tradition and change.

First Minister

Karna

The First Minister of the Lion Kingdom and one of its greatest administrators. Calm, intelligent, and deeply knowledgeable, he oversees governance, law, and royal affairs.

Fig. 5 — Main Villains
Ruler of the Aberrant Dominion

Vyom

The supreme ruler of the Aberrant Dominion and the greatest enemy of humanity. Believing evolution should replace compassion, he seeks to create a perfect world through mutation and biological transformation.

Tiger King · Predator Pact

Vanraj

The powerful Tiger King and founder of the Predator Pact. Driven by pride, loss, and a belief that predators are destined to rule, he becomes Arnav's greatest political rival.

Contract Assassin

Ranga

A legendary contract assassin feared across every continent. Silent, patient, and almost impossible to track, he works only for those who can afford his price.

Fig. 6 — Arnav's Wives
Queen of the Lion Kingdom

Dhriti

Arnav's first queen. A fearless warrior who balances strength with wisdom, she becomes the symbol of unity between humans and the Simha civilization.

Princess of the Garuda Clans

Ira

A master of the skies. Fearless, adventurous, and fiercely independent, she represents freedom, exploration, and the spirit of discovery.

Queen of the Ocean Realms

Varunika

Guardian of the great seas. A gifted navigator and diplomat, she unites the maritime civilizations through cooperation rather than conquest.

Princess of the Naga Houses

Anantha

Guardian of ancient underground knowledge. Calm, intelligent, and deeply spiritual, she preserves secrets that predate modern civilization.

Princess of the Makara Dynasty

Varija

An expert in diplomacy and river trade. Patient and highly strategic, she strengthens the bonds between inland civilizations through negotiation and commerce.

Fig. 7 — Advik's Wives
Commander of the Sacred Forges

Agneya

One of the greatest leaders of the Wolf Clans. She recognizes Advik's genius and helps rebuild the industrial heart of Vaikunth-1 through determination and courage.

Princess of the Ashwa Republic

Ashvini

An exceptional cavalry commander. Fast-thinking, courageous, and disciplined, she represents speed, communication, and the freedom of the open plains.

Scholar of the Hansa Dominion

Vrinda

A respected philosopher known for her wisdom and diplomacy, she believes lasting peace is achieved through knowledge rather than conflict.

Fig. 8 — Atharv's Wives
Archivist of the Kaak Archives

Shyama

Atharv's closest companion. Fascinated by forgotten civilizations and ancient records, she dedicates her life to preserving the world's collective memory.

Guardian of the Rhin Mandalas

Nandita

Defender of the great fortress cities. Strong, dependable, and unwavering, she stands as a symbol of resilience and protection in times of war.

Princess of the Makara Civilization

Varuni

An accomplished diplomat of the river kingdoms. Intelligent and adaptable, she excels at building alliances where others see only conflict.

Fig. 9 — Beyond the Founders

Allies & Antagonists

The brothers don't build their world alone — and they don't do it unopposed. Full profiles for every ally and villain are gathered above.

Fig. 1 — Peoples of Vaikunth-1

Civilizations

Vanara, Simha, and Vyaghra are the first three of twenty-nine civilizations the brothers encounter — allies, rivals, and everything between. Full profiles live on the World of Vaikunth-1 page.

Coming in Full

The Complete Encyclopedia

What's shown here is a small slice of six planned reference volumes covering Vaikunth-1's astrophysics, geography, natural life, civilizations, and religions in full. More of this world will be shared here as it's finished.

Imperial Calendar

The History of Vaikunth-1

A chronological record of Vaikunth-1, from humanity's first landing to the rise of the Empire and Vyom's coming conflict.

Ancient Era

4817-4500 BE

4817 BE - Arrival of the First Team: Humanity's first expedition lands on Vaikunth-1 and establishes the first research outpost.

4800-4760 BE - Mass Immigration Era: Millions of settlers migrate from Earth through successive colony missions.

4760-4700 BE - Settlement Era: Permanent cities, farms, industries, and research facilities are established.

4700-4550 BE - Age of Prosperity: Humanity reaches its first golden age with advanced science, trade, and continental expansion.

4550-4500 BE - Age of Exploration: Every continent and ocean is mapped, distant colonies flourish, and new civilizations emerge.

The Fall of Civilization

4500-2872 BE

4500 BE - The RNA Cataclysm: A catastrophic RNA mutation spreads across the planet, causing the collapse of global civilization.

4500-4000 BE - Age of Collapse: Governments fall, infrastructure is destroyed, and billions perish.

4000-3400 BE - Age of Survival: Small communities struggle against mutants, famine, and the hostile environment.

3400-2872 BE - Age of Rediscovery: Ancient knowledge is slowly recovered as scattered civilizations begin rebuilding.

The Rebirth Era

2872-0 BE

2872-2427 BE - Great War: Humanity wages a prolonged war against powerful mutant empires and corrupted civilizations.

2427-2113 BE - Age of Warlords: Regional military rulers dominate fragmented territories after the Great War.

2113-1714 BE - Rise of Clans: Powerful bloodlines unite people through alliances, marriages, and conquest.

1714-814 BE - Rise of Kingdoms: Stable kingdoms replace clans, creating organized governments, laws, and trade networks.

814-0 BE - Age of Rivalry: Great kingdoms compete through diplomacy, commerce, espionage, and periodic wars while preparing for continental dominance.

The Imperial Era

7 BE onward

7 BE - Fall of Arnav: Arnav suffers his greatest political and personal defeat, nearly ending his ambitions.

0 AE - Making of the Emperor: Arnav unites the realm and is crowned the first Emperor, beginning the Imperial Calendar.

5 AE - Rise of the Grand Alliance: The Empire forms a continent-wide alliance with allied kingdoms and republics.

7 AE - War of Predators: A massive conflict erupts, threatening the stability of the young Empire.

7 AE onward - Imperial Era & Rise of Vyom: The Empire expands across Vaikunth-1 while Vyom rises as its greatest future adversary.

Additional Canon Historical Milestones

Key Dates

4817 BE - First Human Landing

4795 BE - First Permanent City Founded

4728 BE - First Intercontinental Expedition

4689 BE - Discovery of the Padma Ocean Trade Route

4620 BE - Foundation of the First Grand University

4500 BE - RNA Cataclysm

3894 BE - Last Earth Archive Lost

3310 BE - First Rediscovered Library

2872 BE - Beginning of the Great War

2138 BE - Founding of the First Great Clan

1650 BE - First Kingdom Crowned

932 BE - Formation of the Sagar Sangh Maritime League

7 BE - Fall of Arnav

0 AE - Coronation of Emperor Arnav

7 AE - War of Predators

20+ AE - Rise of Vyom and the Dawn of a New Conflict

The Arena of Knowledge

The Founder

About Ayush Kumar Mishra

— Ayush Kumar Mishra

Hello, and welcome. I'm Ayush Kumar Mishra, an aspiring Indian author, worldbuilder, and the creator of Project TRIDENT.

For me, writing has never been just about telling a story. It has always been about creating an entire world that feels real — one with its own geography, history, civilizations, cultures, creatures, politics, and people. I enjoy asking questions like "If humanity had to build a new civilization on another planet, what would it truly look like?" That curiosity became the foundation of Project TRIDENT.

My biggest passion is worldbuilding. Before writing scenes, I often spend countless hours designing maps, shaping continents, naming rivers and mountain ranges, imagining ecosystems, building kingdoms, and creating histories that stretch across generations. I want every location, every civilization, and every character to feel like they belong to a living world rather than simply existing for the plot.

The universe of Vaikunth-1 is the result of that passion. It combines science, adventure, mythology-inspired ideas, exploration, and human resilience into a single fictional world that continues to grow with every revision and every new idea.

I believe stories become memorable when readers feel they are stepping into a place that could exist beyond the pages. That is the experience I hope to create through Project TRIDENT.

Beyond Writing

Curiosity & Craft

Outside writing, I enjoy learning about geography, astronomy, history, and science, because they constantly inspire new ideas for my fictional universe. I also enjoy playing chess, where strategy, patience, and long-term planning challenge the way I think. Gardening reminds me that every meaningful creation grows gradually with care and consistency — much like writing a novel.

The Process

A Journey of Revision

Project TRIDENT has been a journey of continuous learning. I've rewritten ideas, redesigned maps, refined characters, and improved the world many times because I believe every revision makes the story stronger. Building this universe has taught me patience, discipline, and the importance of paying attention to even the smallest details.

Through The Arena of Knowledge, I hope to share not only my novels but also the complete creative universe behind them — from maps and lore to artwork and worldbuilding resources. My goal is to create stories that readers can explore long after they've finished reading. This is only the beginning of that journey, and I'm grateful you've decided to be a part of it.

Welcome to my world.
